title: The Eight Canonical n=6 Number-Theoretic Invariants as an Atlas Primitive Basis
rationale: Selected topic E (canonical nodes) over A (L7_bt promotion), B (hub +277), C (ouroboros taxonomy), D (ndjson bench). E is the strongest scientifically because: (i) it defines a minimal primitive basis (8 integer invariants) that is reproducibly verifiable, (ii) it unifies phase46 (8 nodes) + phase47 (151 bridges, 38 distinct labels) + P1-2 hub growth (+277 with measured load distribution across 7 of 8 primitives) into a single structural story, (iii) every numeric claim maps 1:1 to a committed JSONL file with exact wc/grep derivation, (iv) negative-control removal proves minimality. Topics B and D act as supporting evidence inside §5 and Appendix C rather than as standalone papers.

topic_candidates_considered.A_l7bt_promotion: 108/147 promoted — strong but narrower, essentially a grade-audit result
topic_candidates_considered.B_hub_growth_277: absorbed into §5 EVOLVE of paper E as the load-distribution measurement
topic_candidates_considered.C_ouroboros_13_to_50: modules 13→50 expansion is infrastructure work, not a mathematical theorem
topic_candidates_considered.D_ndjson_bench: engineering optimisation of hexa Stage1 interpreter, lacks mathematical novelty
topic_candidates_considered.E_canonical_nodes: CHOSEN — clean mathematical artifact + measurement + minimality proof

paper_stats.structure: 6 standard sections + 3 appendices (definitions, commit refs, data sources)

tooling.pandoc_xelatex: NOT USED — forbidden by harness H-MD2PDF
